metadata.description: Standard definitions, mandated limits, and regulatory guidelines for Indian health insurance

standard_definitions.cashless_treatment: Treatment where insurer settles bills directly with the network hospital, up to policy limits
standard_definitions.co_payment: A fixed percentage of the admissible claim amount that the insured must bear. Cannot be introduced mid-policy.
standard_definitions.cumulative_bonus: Increase in sum insured by a specified percentage (max 50%) for each claim-free year, without extra premium
standard_definitions.daycare_treatment: Treatment requiring hospitalization of less than 24 hours due to advance medical technology. Covered if listed.
standard_definitions.deductible: Initial specified amount of the claim that the insured must bear before the insurer pays. Can be compulsory or voluntary.
standard_definitions.domiciliary_hospitalization: Treatment at home on doctor's advice when hospital admission is either not possible or not required, continuing for at least 3 days
standard_definitions.grace_period: Time beyond the premium due date (usually 30 days) during which the policy remains in force
standard_definitions.network_hospital: Hospital registered with TPA/insurer for direct settlement of claims. Non-network claims are on reimbursement basis.
standard_definitions.pre_existing_disease: Any condition/ailment/injury/disease diagnosed within 48 months before the first health insurance policy
standard_definitions.pre_hospitalization: Relevant medical expenses incurred during 30-60 days before hospitalization (varies by insurer)
standard_definitions.post_hospitalization: Relevant medical expenses incurred during 60-180 days after discharge (varies by insurer)
standard_definitions.room_rent: Charges for room accommodation during hospitalization. May be subject to sub-limits based on room category.
standard_definitions.sum_insured: Maximum amount payable by insurer in respect of each claim or during the policy period as stated in the policy
standard_definitions.waiting_period: Specified period from the policy start date during which certain defined benefits are not payable
standard_definitions.sub_limit: The maximum payable for a specific item or procedure under the overall sum insured

initial_waiting_period.description: Initial waiting period for any new policy cannot exceed 30 days. Emergency accidents are covered from day 1.

specific_disease_waiting_period.description: Waiting period for specific diseases/procedures cannot exceed 48 months. Most insurers keep 24 months.

pre_existing_disease_waiting_period.description: Pre-existing disease waiting period cannot exceed 48 months. Continuous coverage required.

moratorium_period.description: After 8 continuous years of coverage, insurer cannot contest any claim citing pre-existing disease (except fraud)

permanent.name: Cosmetic/Plastic surgery
permanent.exception: Unless necessitated by injury/illness or as part of medically necessary treatment

permanent.name: Treatment related to change of gender

conditional.name: Mental illness/psychiatric treatment
conditional.note: Must be covered under Mental Healthcare Act 2017

conditional.name: HIV/AIDS treatment
conditional.note: Cannot be excluded if contracted through medical procedure

room_rent_guidelines.description: IRDAI allows insurers to set room rent sub-limits but mandates transparency

common_structures.description: Room rent capped at 1% of sum insured per day
Fixed daily room rent cap in INR
Coverage limited to specific room category (e.g., Single Private AC room)
If room rent exceeds cap, ALL other charges may be proportionally reduced

room_rent_guidelines.proportional_deduction_formula: Payable = (Eligible Room Rent / Actual Room Rent) × Other Charges

types.description: Policyholder chooses higher copay for lower premium
Insurer mandates copay for certain conditions
Higher copay in tier-1 cities (non-network hospitals)
Higher copay for specific chronic diseases

copay_guidelines.regulation: Copay percentage and conditions must be clearly stated in policy document
